caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is supplied at five-digit 62134 rather than six-digit 621340.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Clinical-task fractions exclude a claim that AI replaces billable hands-on treatment.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Audiology and other residual subsegments may have different exposure.
inputs.pi_dist: CMS Medicare mechanics are a direct payer channel but do not describe every commercial or Medicaid contract.
inputs.pi_moat: Sector consolidation evidence does not prove moat for a clinic whose referrals are physician-personal or payer-concentrated.
inputs.t50_years: Vendor survey language indicates direction, not a representative effective-adoption series.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No reopened source provides a code-specific percentage of clinics using AI effectively.
inputs.historical_analogs: The source is a vendor survey release and not a causal clinical-productivity study.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Workforce age and owner age are different populations.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not convert therapist vacancy evidence into owner-succession evidence.
inputs.active_consolidators: Platform count does not establish active acquisition appetite for every local specialty or payer mix.

inputs.buy_mult: S4 comparables are materially larger and mostly historical, so this is a conservative bounded estimate rather than an observed band-size transaction multiple. Verify local transaction data and normalize owner-therapist pay.
inputs.exit_mult: The cited transactions had $12M-$175M EBITDA and cannot support a precise multiple for a smaller clinic group.
inputs.pricing_structure: CMS is not a full description of commercial, Medicaid, school or audiology economics.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1580 (margins.json[62]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Hospitals/Healthcare Facilities EBITDA/Sales 15.80% (closest named bucket for provider economics; public support-services bucket at 3.87% reflects a different business model)); revenue/employee $97,549 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 621340); class <5: 19,498 firms, avg revenue/firm $219,208 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $34,635 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 5,169 firms, avg revenue/firm $664,181 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $104,941 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 3,624 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,193,863 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $188,630 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 2,575 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,111,227 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $491,574 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 460 firms, avg revenue/firm $13,045,304 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,061,158 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 361 firms, avg revenue/firm $53,374,413 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,433,157 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 821

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Documentation, administrative and revenue-cycle work are AI-exposed, whereas licensed, hands-on clinical time remains the paid product. Reclaimed documentation time is a throughput opportunity rather than a clinical headcount assumption.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 621340 ($25,528,793,330) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 621340 ($45,314,241,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. Reopened sources support the payer-capture channel, administrative AI surface, licensed clinical floor, staffing pressure and active consolidation direction, while the scorecard keeps clinical replacement, capture and terminal demand separate. The pass thesis remains useful, but adoption and small-clinic multiple values are disclosed estimates anchored to vendor evidence and much larger historical transactions, so all authored caveats must remain visible.

sources.title: CMS Therapy Services
sources.what_it_supports: Medicare therapy payment mechanics, including the 85% payment rate for qualifying PTA/OTA-furnished services and modifiers.

sources.title: WebPT Releases 2025 State of Rehab Therapy Report
sources.what_it_supports: Rehab-therapy survey context on documentation burden and increasing interest in technology/AI to reduce operational friction.

sources.title: APTA Report Points to Hiring Challenges for Outpatient Practices Amid Growth
sources.what_it_supports: Outpatient PT-practice vacancy rates, job openings and labor scarcity context.

sources.title: Provident Perspectives: Investment & Consolidation in the Physical Therapy Sector
sources.what_it_supports: Historical PT platform transaction multiple range and the consolidation/fragmentation narrative; target EBITDA sizes are materially above most intended targets.

sources.title: Hinge Health claims analysis on fully insured populations
sources.what_it_supports: Company-sponsored, consultant-validated claims analysis reporting reduced utilization including physical or occupational therapy; evidence of a bounded digital-MSK substitution channel.

sources.title: A Physical Therapy Profile: Demographics of the Profession, 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Professional workforce demographic and local-shortage context; it does not report a private-practice owner age distribution.

title: Offices of Physical, Occupational and Speech Therapists, and Audiologists
